segments.text: Then Job replied:
Doubtless you are the only people who matter, and wisdom will die with you!
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: But I have a mind as well as you; I am not inferior to you. Who does not know all these things?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: I have become a laughingstock to my friends, though I called on God and he answered— a mere laughingstock, though righteous and blameless!
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Those who are at ease have contempt for misfortune as the fate of those whose feet are slipping.
The tents of marauders are undisturbed, and those who provoke God are secure— those God has in his hand.
But ask the animals, and they will teach you, or the birds in the sky, and they will tell you;
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: or speak to the earth, and it will teach you, or let the fish in the sea inform you.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Which of all these does not know that the hand of the Lord has done this?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: In his hand is the life of every creature and the breath of all mankind.
Does not the ear test words as the tongue tastes food?
Is not wisdom found among the aged? Does not long life bring understanding?
To God belong wisdom and power; counsel and understanding are his.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: What he tears down cannot be rebuilt; those he imprisons cannot be released.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: If he holds back the waters, there is drought; if he lets them loose, they devastate the land.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: To him belong strength and insight; both deceived and deceiver are his.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: He leads rulers away stripped and makes fools of judges.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: He takes off the shackles put on by kings and ties a loincloth around their waist.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: He leads priests away stripped and overthrows officials long established.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: He silences the lips of trusted advisers and takes away the discernment of elders.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: He pours contempt on nobles and disarms the mighty.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: He reveals the deep things of darkness and brings utter darkness into the light.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: He makes nations great, and destroys them; he enlarges nations, and disperses them.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: He deprives the leaders of the earth of their reason; he makes them wander in a trackless waste.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: They grope in darkness with no light; he makes them stagger like drunkards.
segments.speaker: Job
